source: NORS Table 2 complaint code guidance - curated app guidance

items.label: Abuse: physical
items.use_when: Use when the concern is intentional use of physical force by staff, another resident, family, a representative, a friend, or another person.
items.do_not_use_when: Do not use for non-willful poor care or delay. Use care codes when the issue is care quality, or A05 when gross neglect rather than intentional force is the better fit.
items.human_review: Confirm whether the reported conduct was intentional physical force and identify the alleged perpetrator type when coding.
items.source: NORS Table 2 complaint code guidance - curated app guidance

items.label: Abuse: psychological
items.use_when: Use when the concern is intimidation, threats, humiliation, verbal cruelty, or similar psychological mistreatment.
items.do_not_use_when: Do not use for ordinary rudeness or insensitivity without abuse-level mistreatment. Use D03 for less severe dignity and respect concerns.
items.human_review: Review whether the reported conduct rises to psychological abuse or is better treated as dignity/respect.
items.source: NORS Table 2 complaint code guidance - curated app guidance

items.label: Financial exploitation
items.use_when: Use when someone illegally or improperly uses a resident's funds, property, or assets for another person's profit or advantage.
items.do_not_use_when: Do not use for facility billing, charges, or payment disputes without misuse of resident assets.
items.human_review: Separate resident-asset misuse from billing or trust-fund administration issues.
items.source: NORS Table 2 complaint code guidance - curated app guidance

items.label: Gross neglect
items.use_when: Use when failure to meet essential care needs threatens the resident's health, safety, or welfare.
items.do_not_use_when: Do not use for an isolated lesser care failure that is better coded under a specific F-code.
items.human_review: Use A05 only when the failure to meet essential care threatens health, safety, or welfare.
items.source: NORS Table 2 complaint code guidance - curated app guidance

items.label: Discharge or eviction
items.use_when: Use when the resident received a discharge notice and does not want to leave, was transferred or discharged without notice or due process, was not advised of bed-hold or readmission rules, or was sent to an inappropriate place.
likely_phrases: sent to hospital and won't take me back
items.do_not_use_when: Do not use for an intra-facility room move or roommate issue. Use C04 for room issues.
items.human_review: Confirm whether the issue is discharge, transfer, readmission, bed-hold, notice, or appeal rights.
items.source: NORS Table 2 complaint code guidance - curated app guidance

items.label: Response to requests for assistance
items.use_when: Use when staff fail to respond promptly to call lights, call bells, or other requests for help.
likely_phrases: no response when I asked for transfer help
items.do_not_use_when: Do not use when the delay is retaliatory after a complaint. Use D06 for retaliation.
items.human_review: Repeated dangerous nonresponse may need review for A05 gross neglect.
items.source: NORS Table 2 complaint code guidance - curated app guidance

items.label: Care planning
items.use_when: Use when the facility does not create or follow a person-centered care plan, excludes the resident or representative, or staff disregard the plan.
likely_phrases: plan says two-person assist but they ignore it
items.do_not_use_when: Do not use when the issue is broader care delivery without a care-plan problem. Use the more specific care code when one fits better.
items.human_review: Look for a plan, meeting, participation, implementation, or service mismatch fact.
items.source: NORS Table 2 complaint code guidance - curated app guidance

items.label: Medications
items.use_when: Use when medication is given in error, not given on time or at all, not documented correctly, not secured, expired, mislabeled, or not filled timely.
items.do_not_use_when: Do not use when the concern is mainly choice of provider. Use D01 for provider choice.
items.human_review: Psychotropic medication, restraint, and treatment-rights issues may need additional review.
items.source: NORS Table 2 complaint code guidance - curated app guidance

items.label: Dignity and respect
items.use_when: Use when the resident is treated with rudeness, indifference, or insensitivity.
items.do_not_use_when: Do not use when the conduct rises to abuse. Use A-codes when abuse is the better fit. If the core problem is unanswered requests for help, use F02.
items.human_review: Separate dignity/respect concerns from abuse, privacy, and response-to-assistance concerns.
items.source: NORS Table 2 complaint code guidance - curated app guidance

items.label: Visitors
items.use_when: Use when the facility restricts the resident's ability to choose who to associate with and when to visit, in the facility or community.
items.do_not_use_when: Do not use when the issue is privacy during a visit rather than access to visitors. Use D04 for privacy concerns.
items.human_review: Confirm whether the issue is visitor access, private visiting, association, or a separate privacy concern.
items.source: NORS Table 2 complaint code guidance - curated app guidance

items.label: Privacy
items.use_when: Use when the facility fails to ensure privacy during care or treatment, or in other aspects of life such as confidential information, mail, phone, email, or ombudsman meetings.
items.do_not_use_when: Do not use for broader dignity/respect concerns without a privacy component. Use D03. If interference is with ombudsman access, review B03.
items.human_review: Identify the privacy setting: care, information, mail, phone, email, visit, or ombudsman contact.
items.source: NORS Table 2 complaint code guidance - curated app guidance

items.label: Food services
items.use_when: Use when food quantity, quality, variety, choice, temperature, or timing of meals or snacks is substandard or does not meet resident expectations.
items.do_not_use_when: Do not use when the main issue is staff assistance with eating or drinking. Use H02. If the problem is willful food deprivation, review A01 or A05.
items.human_review: Separate food quality or timing from dining assistance and hydration support.
items.source: NORS Table 2 complaint code guidance - curated app guidance

items.label: Dining and hydration
items.use_when: Use when staff fail to assist with dining or adequate hydration, including adaptive equipment, tray setup, reminders, fluids, or direct assistance.
likely_phrases: needs feeding help but waits too long
items.do_not_use_when: Do not use when the issue is food quality, variety, or timing rather than assistance. Use H01.
items.human_review: If deprivation is willful or rises to essential-care failure, review A01 or A05.
items.source: NORS Table 2 complaint code guidance - curated app guidance

items.label: Staffing
items.use_when: Use when the complaint is shortage of staff, turnover, overuse of agency staff, or inadequate staff skill or training.
items.do_not_use_when: Do not use when the problem is specifically an absent or unresponsive administrator. Use J01.
items.human_review: Use the more specific care code too when staffing caused a concrete care failure.
items.source: NORS Table 2 complaint code guidance - curated app guidance

items.label: Response to complaints
items.use_when: Use when staff ignore or trivialize a resident complaint, there is no grievance process, or the grievance process is not followed or made known.
items.do_not_use_when: Do not use when the harm after complaining is retaliation. Use D06.
items.human_review: Separate failure to respond to complaints from retaliation after a complaint.
items.source: NORS Table 2 complaint code guidance - curated app guidance

items.label: Choice in health care
items.use_when: Use when the resident is denied the right to choose physician, pharmacy, hospice, or other provider, or the resident's preferred care or treatment schedule is not accommodated.
likely_phrases: won't let me keep my doctor
med time not consistent with my routine
items.do_not_use_when: Do not use when the problem is about a service delivered by an outside provider not arranged by the facility.
items.human_review: Review whether provider choice, treatment choice, or schedule choice is the main concern.
items.source: NORS Table 2 complaint code guidance - curated app guidance

items.label: Other rights and preferences
items.use_when: Use when the deprivation involves a resident right not already defined in D01-D08, such as personal liberty, broader freedom of choice, assembly, speech, religion, voting, cohabitation, smoking or non-smoking, or refusal of care or treatment.
likely_phrases: won't let me go to worship
items.do_not_use_when: Do not use when a more specific D-code fits, especially D04 privacy, D05 grievances, or D07 visitors.
items.human_review: Use as a fallback for rights and preferences only after checking more specific D-codes.
items.source: NORS Table 2 complaint code guidance - curated app guidance
